name: Gabby Giffords
subject: re: tonight’s Senate vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
No. Matter. What. | Friend --

Fear is what my constituents felt as a man fired his gun at my congressional event in a Safeway parking lot, shooting me point-blank in the head and killing six others, including a 9-year-old-girl.

Fear is what the children at Sandy Hook Elementary felt as a gunman went shooting from room to room, killing 26 first graders and educators.

Fear is what the clubgoers at Pulse nightclub in Orlando felt as a murderer committed the worst mass shooting in modern U.S. history, killing 49 people just last week.

Earlier this evening, the U.S. Senate voted down two pieces of commonsense gun violence prevention legislation. The senators who voted no will say a lot of things about the votes they cast, and perhaps they might even say they were afraid. But make no mistake, their fear was nothing compared to what far too many victims of gun violence have faced. Theirs was a decision based on political calculations.

I know that if we elect Hillary Clinton our next president, she will not fear the gun lobby. She will do everything in her power to make our communities safer from gun violence. No. Matter. What.

Speaking is still physically difficult for me, but my feelings are clear: I am furious. And I want to ask you directly:

**Please make a contribution to Hillary Clinton today so that she can become our next president, take on the gun lobby, and pass commonsense legislation that will make our communities safer from gun violence.** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjU3ODg1ODkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIzMTE5Mjg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Gabby Giffords

name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Please share your thoughts (seriously)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Would love to know what's on your mind | Friend --

It's time to start a big, new conversation on this campaign -- and what you have to say is pretty much indispensable.

A lot has happened since we started this campaign last April -- we worked hard, had some good days (and maybe a few bad ones), and came together to win a primary and made history! To make sure we're all headed into the general with our best foot forward, we wanted to dig in and ask: What went well for you? What could we have done better?

**[Take this quick 10-question survey.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjU3OTI5MzkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIzMjEwMDYzS0&mt=1&rt=0)** Your feedback will directly impact how we make decisions in the general.

We also don’t want the dialogue to stop there. Over the course of the week, we will be hosting a bunch of events online with different members of our staff -- with all the new folks joining our team, we want to make sure everyone has a chance to talk about where we’ve been and where we’re going. We will keep you posted on those details so you can participate.

**This campaign wouldn’t be the same without you. We’re lucky you’re here and for your continued support as we head into this next phase of this campaign. Please share your thoughts (and in the coming days, we’ll share ours):** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjU3OTI5MzkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIzMjEwMDYzS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America

name: Rob Portman
subject: Hey, Peter
body:
| Hello Peter, Last month, I announced that my campaign has recruited over 500 summer interns to supplement our field program. These interns have become a crucial part of my campaign. As we assemble a larger general election campaign team, we’ve expanded the intern program for the summer and the fall. I can tell for the few short weeks that we have had these interns, they have made a tremendous difference on the campaign. Just last week, our campaign contacted _179,321 swing voters_! **I am very impressed with how hard these young men and women are working. They have become a very important part of my campaign,** which is why I have decided to bring on 12 more interns for the summer and the fall. Peter, will you help me fund the internship program on my campaign? **[I must raise roughly $3,000 to expand our grassroots effort. Each intern’s literature and door-to-door efforts costs roughly $250, perhaps you could sponsor an intern for $250?](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fcontribute.robportman.com%2Fcontribute_first%3Fcampaign_id%3D0706c4d4f2697a5cd40bf9c07e1472ab%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26utm_campaign%3Deoq_nondonor_02&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=eoq_nondonor_02&n=1 "Donate Here!") **We also must raise money to pay for some of their campaign expenses such as campaign supplies, gas, food, etc. [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fcontribute.robportman.com%2Fcontribute_first%3Fcampaign_id%3D0706c4d4f2697a5cd40bf9c07e1472ab%26email%3Ddpwillis67%40gmail.com%26utm_campaign%3Deoq_nondonor_02&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=eoq_nondonor_02&n=2 "Donate Here!")

name: Russ Feingold
subject: One vote
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
We need a fully functioning Supreme Court. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf432d/1c469400/6fef134/356a653/4017617729/VEsH/ "http://www.signforgood.com/doyourjob/?code=feingold") Friend, The Supreme Court will issue decisions this week that will directly affect the lives of millions of Americans. The court will decide the fate of President Obama's immigration actions, it could rule on the future of affirmative action programs at public and private universities, and it'll issue a decision on a landmark reproductive rights case in Texas, either affirming or denying Texas Republicans' efforts to shutter abortion clinics across the state. But because of gridlock in the Republican Senate -- these landmark decisions will be determined by an eight-person court, and not to a fully functioning nine-person court. Because of Republican obstructionism, a one-vote difference could change the lives of so many Wisconsinites, and millions of Americans. Today, as the court prepares to hear three landmark cases, we must urgently renew the call for the Senate to consider President Obama's nominee Merrick Garland to the Supreme Court. **[Add your name, right now, to urge Senate Republicans to do their job and consider President Obama's nominee to the Supreme Court.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf432d/1c469400/6fef134/356a653/4017617729/VEsE/ "http://www.signforgood.com/doyourjob/?code=feingold")** It's been over four months since Justice Scalia's passing. Yet we have seen no action -- zero -- from a Republican Senate on their constitutional duty to consider the president's nominee. But when I talk to Wisconsinites? They want a vote. That's what I'm hearing. That's why it's so important that we keep up the pressure -- that we stand together to call on Republicans to do their job by considering the president's nominee. **[Add your name now, and urge Republicans to consider and vote on Merrick Garland's nomination.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf432d/1c469400/6fef134/356a653/4017617729/VEsF/ "http://www.signforgood.com/doyourjob/?code=feingold")** Thank you, Russ Feingold | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf432d/1c469400/6fef134/356a653/4017617729/VEsC/ "http://www.signforgood.com/doyourjob/?code=feingold")

clean_body: We need a fully functioning Supreme Court. | "" Friend, The Supreme Court will issue decisions this week that will directly affect the lives of millions of Americans. The court will decide the fate of President Obama's immigration actions, it could rule on the future of affirmative action programs at public and private universities, and it'll issue a decision on a landmark reproductive rights case in Texas, either affirming or denying Texas Republicans' efforts to shutter abortion clinics across the state. But because of gridlock in the Republican Senate -- these landmark decisions will be determined by an eight-person court, and not to a fully functioning nine-person court. Because of Republican obstructionism, a one-vote difference could change the lives of so many Wisconsinites, and millions of Americans. Today, as the court prepares to hear three landmark cases, we must urgently renew the call for the Senate to consider President Obama's nominee Merrick Garland to the Supreme Court. "" It's been over four months since Justice Scalia's passing. Yet we have seen no action -- zero -- from a Republican Senate on their constitutional duty to consider the president's nominee. But when I talk to Wisconsinites? They want a vote. That's what I'm hearing. That's why it's so important that we keep up the pressure -- that we stand together to call on Republicans to do their job by considering the president's nominee. "" Thank you, Russ Feingold | ""

- Associated Press, June 21_

Toomey has made his priorities clear -- he's with the NRA, not the American people.

[**Friend, we need to send him a message.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/1921865086046044160/-4533693260622725120)

Last night -- after days of empty talk -- Toomey voted **_AGAINST_** two bipartisan amendments that would have closed the terror gun loophole and expanded background checks on gun purchases. There is no excuse for this failed leadership.

[**We need common-sense gun measures, and we need them now. Add your name today to call on Pat Toomey to back up his words with action.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/1921865086046044160/-4533693260622725120)

This shouldn't be a tough decision: 92% of Americans want expanded background checks, and 85% would ban those on federal watchlists from buying guns.

But the NRA spent more than $1 million to help buy Toomey's Senate seat, so it's no surprise he picked their side when we really needed him. Pennsylvanians deserve better -- let's show Toomey and the NRA we won't stand for it any longer.

[**Sign your name now.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/1921865086046044160/-4533693260622725120)

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: thanks for taking action
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
|

Friend,

Thanks to friends and supporters like you friend, for helping us reach our goal last week to support our Maryland Rapid Response Fund.

Your help has been critical in ensuring we have the resources to stand up to the right wing SuperPAC funds coming our way. With wealthy GOP contributors like the Koch Brothers now ready to spend millions on down-ballot statewide races like ours here in Maryland, we can't afford to let our guard down.

This week we're waiting for one of the most important Supreme Court decisions since _Roe v. Wade_. Meanwhile Senate Republicans are once again voting down common sense gun legislation, refusing to permit hearings on the President's Supreme Court nominee, and hoping the highest court of the land will uphold a Texas law designed to restrict women's right to choose by forcing women's health clinics to close. It could not be clearer what we are fighting for.

[_**And that 's why I'm asking you to join me on a critical issue - standing up for women's health care. Can you add your name to say its time we move FORWARD, not BACKWARDS on women's health?**_](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ddf/70cdc2ba/47f4f6a1/3972229/2942419159/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stand-up-for-women-s-health-scotus?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+And+thatrsquos+why+Irsquom+asking+you+to&utm_campaign=em062116&source=em062116")

Any day now the eight Supreme Court justices are expected to rule on the _Whole Women 's Health v. Hellerstedt _case. Their decision will determine whether a state can restrict health care clinic services for women. If the justices uphold the lower court, millions of women in 22 states could be denied access to proper health care.

Forty three years after _Roe v. Wade_ it's hard to believe that a woman's access to healthcare could be up for debate. But that's where right-wing extremists in the Republican party want to go. And that's what we are fighting against.

[**Please add your name and send right-wing extremists a clear message: EVERY woman has the right to safe and legal health care, no matter where she lives.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ddf/70cdc2ba/47f4f6a1/3972226/2942419159/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stand-up-for-women-s-health-scotus?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Please+add+your+name+and+send+rightwing+&utm_campaign=em062116&source=em062116")

Thanks for your support. There's a lot at stake in 2016 and if we stand together I know we can get the job done.

Chris
---

Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
---

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I’m with her. I need you to be, too
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Hillary is a fighter | Friend --

I haven’t been in the Senate for long, but I’ve learned: If you don’t fight, you can’t win. And I’ll be honest, when things get tough, too often our side just folds and gives up.

But not Hillary Clinton. For 25 years, she’s been on the receiving end of attack after attack. She didn’t whimper. She didn’t whine. She’s always fought back with grace and determination – and no matter how many punches she took, each time she came out fighting stronger.

**[This is one of reasons I’m with Hillary -- and if you’re with her too, chip in to get your free sticker right now.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjU3OTY3NzgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIzMjM5MDEwS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

Hillary is a fighter. Throughout this campaign, she has said she’ll hold Wall Street accountable, raise the minimum wage, protect and expand Social Security, and help students graduate from college without being buried in debt. She’ll fight for women’s rights and LGBT rights and immigrant rights and civil rights. That’s Hillary’s agenda, and it is a progressive agenda.

We need a candidate who fights for the right values -- and who isn’t afraid to fight back against right-wing lunatics trying to undermine progress in our country. Because let’s be honest, the Republicans have nominated the looniest of the right-wing lunatics to become our country’s next Commander-in-Chief.

Donald Trump is a man who built his campaign on racism, sexism, and xenophobia. A man who got rich while declaring bankruptcy, skipping out on what he owed to others, and scamming people. A man who is a small, insecure, moneygrubbing bully who doesn’t care who gets hurt, so long as he makes a buck off it. A man who should never, ever be allowed to set foot in the White House.

So here’s the deal: Hillary is smart as a whip, and she’s a tough cookie. She can take Donald Trump’s attacks and nasty name-calling. But if she’s going to win in November – if we are going to win in November – then she cannot do it alone.

**It’s going to take all of us fighting hard to make sure Donald Trump gets NOWHERE near the White House. Hillary will fight her heart out to level the playing field for working families. That’s why I’m with her -- and that’s why we all need to be with her:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjU3OTY3NzgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIzMjM5MDEwS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth Warren

|
**Hillary needs you now more than ever -- chip in $1 to join more than 1.3 million grassroots donors standing with her:**

name: Jay Inslee
subject: We need this now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We need to fix wildfire funding. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTA5MjI4OQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQyMDgwMDU3S0&mt=1&rt=0) | Dear Friend, As we all know, 2014 and 2015 were disastrous years for wildfires in Washington. The aftermath left families and their loved ones around the state devastated. Washington has had a front row seat to some of the most harmful impacts of climate change. That is simply unacceptable. We are working to improve our climate through clean air and energy initiatives, but we have to do more to address wildfires. We can start by fixing the way the federal government funds wildfire response. Here's something that makes no sense: **The National Forest Service is forced to use funds that are supposed to be dedicated to wildfire _prevention_ to fund wildfire emergency response.** It's like the old idiom of robbing Peter to pay Paul. This art is called "fire borrowing." Funds from prevention efforts are being raided in order to respond to disaster situations. That's just not the right way to do this. Prevention of future wildfires is more important than ever, and we're hampering our future mitigation efforts in order to fight fires today. I've demanded a solution to this problem by calling for a fix at the federal level. [**Add your name today to demand a fix to federal wildfire response funding. We need to solve this now.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTA5MjI4OQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQyMDgwMDU3S0&mt=1&rt=0) I've written to Majority Leader Mitch McConnell and Speaker Paul Ryan pressing for a fix to this problem, and I've been joined by members of Washington's congressional delegation. Wildfires should be treated the same way as floods and hurricanes are in federal accounting. It's entirely possible -- and it's entirely necessary. [**I hope you 'll join me to urge the federal government to fix its wildfire response funding model. Add your name today.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTA5MjI4OQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQyMDgwMDU3S0&mt=1&rt=0) Washington can't afford to wait any longer for a fix. We need this now. Thank you for taking action. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [**TAKE ACTION »**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=OTA5MjI4OQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQyMDgwMDU3S0&mt=1&rt=0)
